name: Microsoft/ms-import-history-soft-delete
description: $import version/history conflict-resolution conformance tests (from ImportHistorySoftDeleteTests.cs): client-assigned meta.versionId/meta.lastUpdated and soft-delete interleaving during ndjson ingestion, exclusive to the $import pipeline and only observable after a completed real import. All 5 source tests are skipped for that reason; this suite only checks that the mode/allowNegativeVersions request shape itself is accepted (best-effort).

test.name: $import kickoff shape accepts mode=IncrementalLoad and allowNegativeVersions (best-effort, environment-dependent)
test.description: A $import kickoff with mode=IncrementalLoad and allowNegativeVersions=true should return 202 Accepted with a Content-Location header; both assertions are warningOnly since reaching 202 depends on the target's real storage account, which this suite can't know.

assert.description: 202 Accepted if the input URL happens to match the server's configured storage account (rarely true for a portable suite).
400 Bad Request otherwise (not a rejection of mode/allowNegativeVersions themselves), which is the far more likely outcome.
